coreJobStatement: Design, manufacture, and commission rail vehicles that transport passengers and freight safely, reliably, and on schedule
outputTypes: High-speed passenger EMU/DMU (ICE, TGV, Shinkansen, Velaro)
Regional/intercity passenger cars (commuter EMU, intercity coaches)
Freight wagons (flatcars, boxcars, tank wagons, hoppers)
hierarchy: Process VN (L7 through L4) — rail transportation equipment manufacturing chain
marketSize: ~$16B US (2017); ~$20B US est. 2025; ~161 US establishments; ~29,400 employees; Top OEMs: Alstom, Siemens Mobility, Stadler, CAF, Hitachi Rail, CRRC

l6Systems.name: Vehicle Design & Type Approval
l6Systems.jobFamily: Define vehicle architecture, system engineering, and certify design for homologation

l6Systems.name: Carbody Fabrication
l6Systems.jobFamily: Fabricate load-bearing vehicle shell and underframe from structural materials

l6Systems.name: Bogie & Running Gear Manufacturing
l6Systems.jobFamily: Produce running gear that interfaces vehicle with track and transfers loads

l6Systems.name: Mechanical Systems Integration
l6Systems.jobFamily: Install non-electrical vehicle systems: doors, HVAC, couplers, pneumatics, gangways

l6Systems.name: Electrical & Propulsion Integration
l6Systems.jobFamily: Install traction drives, power electronics, onboard electrical distribution

l6Systems.name: Interior Fit-Out
l6Systems.jobFamily: Install passenger-facing elements: seating, wall panels, flooring, accessibility systems

l6Systems.name: Final Assembly & System Integration
l6Systems.jobFamily: Assemble all subsystems; verify whole-vehicle performance; pre-commissioning

l6Systems.name: Pre-delivery Testing & Homologation
l6Systems.jobFamily: Commission, test, and certify vehicle for railway operating environment (TSI, EN 12663)

l6Systems.name: Quality Assurance & Weld Inspection (H)
l6Systems.jobFamily: EN 15085-CL1 certified weld inspection and quality management across all core steps

vnUnits.name: Structural Steel Profiles
vnUnits.functionalJob: ZOLLERN PRIMARY POSITION. Hot-rolled custom profiles for bogie sills, floor beams, door frames, body pillars (~40%). Standard hot-rolled structural sections (~35%). Aluminum alloy extrusions (~20%, not ZOLLERN - C6 constraint). Stainless steel roll-formed (~5%).
vnUnits.description: Hot-rolled and cold-drawn steel profiles for carbody structures, bogie frames, door frames, running gear interfaces

vnUnits.name: Carbody Sheet & Plate
vnUnits.functionalJob: High-strength carbon/alloy steel S355, S420N (~65%). Stainless steel sheet (~25%). Aluminum alloy sheet (~10%).

vnUnits.name: Carbody Welding & Fabrication
vnUnits.functionalJob: Robotic MAG/MIG welding (~60%, EN 15085-CL1 certified). Laser welding (~25%). Manual/semi-auto welding (~15%).

vnUnits.name: Bogie Frame Profiles
vnUnits.functionalJob: ZOLLERN SECONDARY POSITION. Side beam / longeron and cross beam / headstock profiles for bogie frame fabrication. Hot-rolled structural sections in S355, 42CrMo4.

zollernPosition: ZOLLERN holds PRIMARY position at L5 Structural Steel Profiles under L6b Carbody Fabrication, and SECONDARY position at Bogie Frame Profiles under L6c. Profile types: floor beams, solesbars, roof rails, body pillars, door frames (carbody); side beams, cross beams (bogie).
strategicPosition: Certification-intensive market (EN 15085-CL1, EN 12663, TSI). ZOLLERN's EN 10204 3.1 traceability and IATF 16949 infrastructure are key differentiators. Constraint C10 (corrosion) mitigable via weathering steel or zinc-rich primer.
